---
title: "Choosing an ICC"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Choosing an ICC}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
```

## The decision framework

The flagship teaching article. It will guide you through the choices that define
*which* ICC you need:

- **Absolute agreement vs. consistency** — does the actual value matter, or only
  rank order?
- **Single vs. average** — are you reporting one rater, or the mean of *k*?
- **Fixed vs. random raters** — are your raters the whole population of interest,
  or a sample you generalize from?
- **Complete vs. incomplete** — is every subject rated by every rater?
- **Subject- vs. cluster-level** — where does the object of measurement sit?

> This is a placeholder. The full decision framework is written in **Milestone 3**
> (`project/MILESTONES.md`), the milestone where incomplete designs make the
> choice consequential.
